flow.summary: Keep gym between 74-76°F using hallway thermostat.

steps.text: Is gym temperature between 74-76°F?
No action needed.
Go to thermostat in hallway by the bathrooms. Press down arrow and set a temporary hold until it reaches 74-76°F.
Did the gym cool down to the correct range?
Done — temperature is in range.
Contact management through Supplies & Repairs on Slack. They will resolve it as quickly as possible.

sop_nsi_001.title: No Sweat Intro (NSI) — Sales Floor SOP

flow.summary: Step-by-step process for conducting a No Sweat Intro with a prospect.

steps.text: Greet them by name at the door. Ask: 'Hey, what brings you in today?'
Did they give a clear goal or a vague answer?
Keep asking 'Why?' until you reach the real pain point. Share a member story with the same pain point.
Follow up with 'Why?' and keep digging until you find the deeper motivation.
Explain CrossFit if asked. Focus on community, coaching, and results. Tour the facility.
Present membership options. Always present highest tier first. Ask: 'Which of these feels like the best fit?'
Did they sign up?
Complete intake, schedule first session, introduce to Wodify app.
Handle objection. Address concern, offer a trial class, or schedule a follow-up. Add to Lead Machine.

flow.summary: Steps to open the gym each morning before the first class.

steps.text: Arrive at least 15 minutes before class. Use Kisi app to unlock the front gate (code: 3 and 5 simultaneously then 1).
Turn on all lights. Check gym temperature — should be 74-76°F.
Turn on music via Sonos app. Set to appropriate volume for pre-class energy.
Check equipment is set up for the day's workout. Verify whiteboard has the WOD written.
Check bathrooms — paper towels, soap, toilet paper stocked. Spray and wipe down surfaces if needed.
Open Wodify on your device. Confirm class roster and any special notes for athletes.
Gym is open and ready. Greet athletes as they arrive.

flow.summary: What to do when equipment is damaged or broken.

steps.text: Is the equipment a safety hazard (sharp edges, unstable, could injure someone)?
Remove from gym floor immediately. Place in storage or mark clearly as OUT OF SERVICE.
Tag the item so other coaches know it is broken. Do not remove from floor if still usable.
Post in Slack #supplies-and-repairs with: item name, description of damage, photo if possible.
Management will arrange repair or replacement. Check back if not resolved within one week.

flow.summary: Daily trash takeout and scheduled trash day procedures.

steps.text: Is the trash bin more than half full or does it smell?
Replace trash bag. Tie off full bag and take to dumpster outside.
Check again after next class. Do not leave overfull bins overnight.
Is today trash pickup day?
Bring dumpster to the curb before 7AM. Bring back in after pickup.
Trash replaced. No further action needed.

flow.summary: How to restock soap, paper towels, snacks, and other consumables.

steps.text: Is the item completely out or running low (less than 20% remaining)?
Is there backup stock in the supply closet?
Restock from supply closet. Note what was used so management can reorder.
Post in Slack #supplies-and-repairs: item name, quantity needed, urgency level.
For snack fridge (apples, oranges, drinks): order through H-E-B or Element/O2 per management SOP.
Item is stocked or order has been placed.

flow.summary: How to open and close the front gate and gym doors.

steps.text: Are you opening or closing?
OPENING: Use Kisi app to unlock gate. Code is 3 and 5 simultaneously then 1.
Is the gate stuck or not responding?
Try manual keypad. If still not working, post in Slack #supplies-and-repairs immediately.
CLOSING: Ensure all athletes have left. Check bathrooms and back area.
Turn off all lights. Turn off Sonos. Set thermostat to energy save mode.
Lock front door. Lock gate via Kisi app. Confirm locked before leaving.
Gate is open. Proceed with opening checklist.
